method: Only allowlisted official applicant-tracking-system feeds are queried. Results are limited to broad U.S. technical and specialty-occupation role families aligned with the federal Standard Occupational Classification framework, including software, AI/ML, data, cloud/SRE, cybersecurity, network and systems, database, QA automation, technical product/program management, solutions engineering, hardware and embedded systems, electrical, mechanical, civil, biotechnology, healthcare technology, quantitative finance, UX/product design and technical writing. A posting must identify a location in one of the 50 U.S. states, Washington, D.C., or explicitly U.S.-based remote work. Categories describe occupations rather than nationality. Sponsorship is classified first from the current posting. When the posting is silent, independently reviewed USCIS H-1B employer history may be shown as a separate historical signal; it is never treated as confirmation that the current role will be sponsored. Closed positions are removed whenever they disappear from the employer's current official feed; newly published matching positions are added during the same refresh.

occupationTaxonomy.label: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics — Standard Occupational Classification
occupationTaxonomy.note: The category expansion is grounded in nationally recognized computer, mathematical, architecture, engineering, science, healthcare technical, business and design occupation families.

federalVisaEvidence.label: USCIS — H-1B Employer Data Hub
federalVisaEvidence.note: USCIS employer-level petition history is informational. A prior H-1B filing or approval does not guarantee sponsorship for a current opening, candidate or work location.

jobTrackingNote: Shared D1-backed confirmation and invalid-report tracking. Three net invalid reports place a job in the follow-up queue and hide it by default.
